content: @Jon : Just confirming my PR for a first cut of the odraw 1.6 manual.

I have incorporated your changes into the latest master (master_1.6 is old and is for a previous version to allow fixing). Can you please update to the latest master and then create a branch from there for any future pull requests. Also, if you add [skip ci] [skip appveyor] to the first line of your commit there will be no 'build' done for the pull request. Thanks

Everything moved to the master branch.
"[skip ci] [skip appveyor] to the first line of your commit"
Hope this worked. Added [skip ci] [skip appveyor] in two places, just in case.

Update using github.io is here:
https://rasbats.github.io/ocpn-manuals-test//ocpn-user-plugins-manual/0.1/index.html

I don't like all the links [0.1] appearing in the left hand area. But this can be dealt with later in the ui-bundle.

Linking within documents is taken care of by the Contents index.

Any changes made by devs to the manual pages in their repo will be reflected in the plugins manual when next built. It does not require me updating my fork. The fork is only used for initial setup and proposed changes via PR.
@Alec Leamas : Do you have a python snippet for Windows that removes all the directories + files from a base directory?
The problem is removing .git folder with files. Permissions.
use shutil, works on WIndows as well. shutil.rmtree() IIRC
You don't have permissions to remove your own .git dir?

OMG: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/12168110/how-to-set-folder-permissions-in-windows

Use a proper OS instead ;)
NOTIME: I removed the .git folder by hand. TBC

Have now pulled everything into one repo to make it easier to track builds and keep everything together.

Demo website is now at:
https://rasbats.github.io/opencpn-plugins-manual/opencpn-plugins-manual/0.1/index.html

Reason for name change is that it scans better. No need for 'user'. opencpn more descriptive than ocpn.

Intend adding the first of the beta plugins. The titles can be otidalplan(beta) for example. Separate beta start page, with link on the first page that takes you to that page.

Thoughts?
Had a quick look, still kind of impressing. Would appreciate if url could be kept stable for a while, it is linked fromthe UI.
I have updated the UI, it now behaves better when the window becomes narrow. You could pick the bundle at https://github.com/leamas/ocpn-manuals
"Separate beta start page, with link on the first page that takes you to that page." Seems like the best solution.
Focus now for me: Reviewing and fixing text in the DM. Most bits in place, but still a lot of work to clean up and discuss with @Rick Gleason

Mike looks good to me. I wonder if you can include Dashboard-Tactics by {Canne} Petri it is on readmedocs.io using sphinx.
https://github.com/canne/dashboard_tactics_pi https://dashboard-tactics-pi.readthedocs.io/en/v2.0.0/

Also I notice your version of watchdog is not up to date, is there something I need to do on this endd?

@Rick Gleason : I will have a look at Dashboard-Tactics and see about translating to asciidoc.
With Watchdog I hope we are level with DokuWiki. Any changes you make will update the plugins manual when a new build is made. Please edit as you see fit.

Thanks MIke, I will update it.
I have been trying to figure out how to make bullets and single lines format closer together. This format takes up lots of space. Antora/acsciidoc does not seem to have much. I suppose it requires some CSS....?

@Rick Gleason : To sava a PR, when you are editing Watchdog could you change the title in antora.yml from 'Watchdog Manual' to 'Watchdog'. This tidies up the list of plugins and avoids confusion with the main manual.
https://github.com/rgleason/watchdog_pi/blob/master/manual/watchdog/antora.yml

@Rick Gleason : This for the syntax:
https://docs.asciidoctor.org/asciidoc/latest/syntax-quick-reference/#ex-ul-max
The page also explains how the text needs this limited font, line spacing etc so that it is capable of outputting pdf, epub etc.

Not much there yet but I have started a wiki to act as a store of useful information (to me anyway).
https://github.com/Rasbats/opencpn-plugins-manual/wiki
Which makes me think some documents are going to be needed to help people update their plugin manual.
@Rick Gleason , @Alec Leamas : what asciidoc editors are you using?

I just use vi (vim) + rebuilding to preview. That is, personally I don't think WYSIWYG is that important here.

vim has som limited asciidoc support, and if I was motivated I know I could find a plugin with still better support. OTOH, using vim is , well, a bit special and very long from windows paradigms.
BTW: If you have git bash installed, you already have also vim installed; it's part of that packaging.
VS Code rules here! Now that I have sorted out the images issue I am knocking out pdf as well as HTML.
When you have some time: https://github.com/Rasbats/managed_plugins/pull/6

That PR was broken, my bad. Sorry. You might want to revert it.
New, better one: https://github.com/Rasbats/managed_plugins/pull/7
Time to spring yacht visit

"Time to spring yacht visit"
Me next week.
BTW - where did you get the image for the translations workflow. Very nice.
OpenCPN website: Have you talked to Pavel/Dave about the hosting side? Is a script possible, to take an HTML build from the repo and move it to a website outside GitHub pages?
BTW - where did you get the image for the translations workflow. Very nice. Did it myself with some help of Dr Google.

OpenCPN website: Have you talked to Pavel/Dave about the hosting side?_ The easiest way would be to use Github Pages, and just put a pointer at opencpn.org. Needs to discuss with Pavel, but it's IMHO too early.

"but it's IMHO too early."
Agree, but I think it needs considering before we get in over our heads. (Maybe already over my head ;))
Deployment is usually tricky, but int this case I don't see any major problems ahead when we start discussing with Pavel

Have been using this opensource diagramming software for a while:
https://github.com/jgraph/drawio-desktop/releases/tag/v14.5.1
Sorry... very WYSIWYG but runs on Win/Lin/Mac
@Jon : Sorry... [skip ci] [skip appveyor] should have been added as the first line in my commit message to my forks of your plugins, not the PR message. Will get it right next time.

@Rick Gleason :
"I wonder if you can include Dashboard-Tactics by {Canne} Petri"
Converting all his restructured text is not going to fly at the moment. I will get the dokuwiki work into his repo if the PR is accepted and leave the link to the 'ReadTheDocs.io' site.
Sounds good to me. It can be fixed later perhaps by Petri.
@Rick Gleason : Bit confused. You have the managed installer repo. Has not been pushed to Petri as far as I can tell. Who wrote the dokuwiki work? Who do I push the manual to?

Yes, for a moment I drew a blank. Yes, I did a stable version of dasboard-tactics as a Managed Plugin I believe (it needs update), but he has version 2.0 now. He is waiting to do an Plugin Manager installer until we get to the next major update and wxwidgets because of issues with some of his helper programs and links, as I understand it. The version I did works with current OpenCPN but does not have the influxDB and graphics in version 2.0. So you would want to push to Petrti [Canne}
Thanks Rick. I will do that.
BTW Petri did not want my push, because he has future plans.

OpenCPN website: Have you talked to Pavel/Dave about the hosting side?_ The easiest way would be to use Github Pages, and just put a pointer at opencpn.org. Needs to discuss with Pavel, but it's IMHO too early.

I wrote a note to him but stopped because you did want to bother him. I think it is time to do it.
but there is another alternative. Make a new github account for documents.
OpenCPN-Docs and use that. Give all of us access Mike, Alec, Rick, Jon, Bdbcat, Pavel.

what asciidoc editors are you using? notepad++
This topic was moved here from #ocpn-user-plugins-manual > PR by Alec Leamas.
